text:
It was a quiet night. Stars peeked through the thin veil of a dusky blue sky. A lone figure, wrapped in midnight black, stood hunched beside a crackling fire. The person's silhouette seemed almost to meld with the flickering flames.

Nameless, the one who remained nameless, had come into this clearing many times under the cover of dark. What they didn't know was that the woods around them were home to more than just the natural symphony of nocturnal creatures. 

In the distance, beyond the familiar grove of ancient oak trees, stirred a movement. A raccoon, its eyes wide with curiosity, sniffed the air. Its tiny paw found the edge of a discarded tin can, and with careful, clawed hands, it popped the top. The contents spilled out—a mix of popcorn kernels and mystery bits.

The raccoon leaned in closer, tilting its head, and popped another kernel into its mouth. As more popped, the satisfaction on
